Off Calendar May-12-2010 Continued To Jun-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trudy Martin for Rose Fitzpatrick. Andrew Jacobson for Jeanette Antonio.

Mr. Jacosbson reported that they have a settlement, stated that Jeanette Antonio agreed to make a portion of her revocable personal trust irrevocable with respect to Anthony Fitzpatrick, to personally take care of Patrick for as long as she is financialy and physicaly able to do so and for as long as Patrick can live in a normal environment, to advise Ms. Fitzpatrick of any changes in circumstances, to make payment to Rose Fitzpatrick in the amount of $5,000.00 which is to be paid over 6 months and that a certification from one of Anthony's Physicians is required in the event that Anthony is no longer able to live in a normal environment. The Court requested a further declaration with regard to Anthony's understanding and ability to agree to the terms of the settlement. The Parties agreed to have the proposed Guradian Ad Litem go over the settlement with Anthony and submit a declaration. All matters including the issue of the need for a Guardian Ad Litem were continued to June 2, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar May-12-2010 Continued To Jun-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Any Remaining Issues
Off Calendar May-05-2010 Continued To May-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trudy Martin for Rose Fitzpatrick (Petitioner). Andrew Jacobson for Jeanette Antonio.

Counsel stated that they were close to a settlement and that they needed more time to work on the issues. With regard to the need for a Guardian Ad Litem for Anthony Fitzpatrick, Mr. Jacobson stated that Anthony was going to work and was acting responsively. Ms. Martin stated that there may be a need to have someone watching over him and that they would work on long term protection being built into the agreement. All matters were continued to May 12, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Aug-12-2008 Continued To Aug-26-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trudy Martin for Rose Fitzpatrick (Petitioner). Andrew Jacobson for Jeanette Antonio (Co-Trustee).

Mr. Jacobson stated that he was unable to complete the revised accounting and requested a two week continuance. Ms. Martin did not object to the continuance and stated that she wanted Mr. Jacobson to address the issues raised in the Examiner's notes. The matter was continued to August 26, 2008 for status on the accounting. Sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will be imposed for everyday that the accounting is not filed after August 26, 2008. Status on the remaining issues was set for October 15, 2008. The Parties will file an ex parte petition for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem for Anthony Fitzpatrick. Ms. Martin objects to Jeanette Antonio or Paul Fitzpatrick being appointed as Guardian Ad Litem.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Off Calendar Mar-12-2008 Continued To May-06-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trudy Martin for Rose Fitzpatrick (Petitioner). Andrew Jacobson for Jeanette Antonio (Trustee).

Mr. Jacobson stated that he submitted the accounting but that he forget to file it. Ms. Martin stated that she was prepared to add objections when the petition is filed. The Parties presented oral arguments. The Court directed Mr. Jacobson to file the accounting petition and give 30 days notice to other interested parties. Objections to the accounting must be filed one week before the hearing date set for the accounting. The matter was continued to May 6, 2008 for status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Bonner.

Petition For Instruction And For Order Compelling Trustee To Account
Off Calendar Feb-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trudy Martin for Rose Fitzpatrick (Petitioner). Andy Jacobson for Jeanette Antonio (Administrator).

Ms. Martin presented copies of the documents filed in opposition to the response to the Court. In addition, she stated that there were two issues: 1) it was appropriate to compel the accounting as the foundational element was met under probate code 12700(b)(7), client has standing. 2) dispute regarding the amount paid for the benefit of the disabled brother. Mr. Jacobson stated that he provided all the documents months ago, and that there were no more documents to produce. In addition, he argued that the Trust closed in 2003 and questioned why they waited so long to bring these issues forward. The Court ordered an accounting to be filed on or before February 27, 2008. Objections are to be filed by March 5, 2008. The attorneys fees were taken under submission. The matter was set for status on March 12, 2008. The parties are to meet and confer.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR#8098. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.
